rationale: An AWS access key matching pattern AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16} was found committed to the main branch of repository api-service in a .env file. Shannon entropy of the key value is 4.7, indicating a real credential. The key is associated with account ci-role-prod which has broad S3 and EC2 access, placing blast radius at full_account. The commit is recent (within 24 hours) and on the production branch, indicating the key may be in active use.
summary: High-confidence AWS access key exposure detected in production branch commit. Immediate rotation and revocation required.
key_findings: AWS access key pattern AKIA matched with entropy 4.7 (threshold: 3.5) — confidence: 0.97
Credential committed to main branch of api-service in .env file (not .env.example)
Associated identity ci-role-prod has S3 and EC2 permissions — blast radius: full_account
Commit timestamp within 24 hours — key likely in active use
No test or placeholder indicators found
recommendation: Immediately rotate and revoke the exposed AWS access key. Notify the responsible team. Audit CloudTrail for any usage of this key since the commit timestamp. Apply a pre-commit hook to prevent future secret commits.
